{"body":"id: GO:0009389
name: dimethyl sulfoxide reductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: dimethyl sulfoxide + H+ = dimethyl sulfide + H2O." [PMID:8658134]
synonym: "dimethyl sulphoxide reductase activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/28261" xsd:anyURI

=> EXP annotated to DmsA/B/C by EcoCyc from PMID:1917829 and PMID:3280546
=> EcoCyc annotates these genes to EC:1.8.5.3/MetaCyc:DIMESULFREDUCT-RXN
=> this is RHEA:28494: dimethyl sulfide + a menaquinone + H2O = dimethyl sulfoxide + a menaquinol

- [x] add these xrefs
- [x] change parent to GO:0016672	oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors, quinone or similar compound as acceptor (EC:1.8.5.-)


----


id: GO:0043914
name: NADPH:sulfur oxidoreductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: NADPH + H+ + sulfur = hydrogen sulfide + NADP+." [GOC:jl, PMID:17449625]
xref: EC:1.8.1.18 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30193" xsd:anyURI

- [x] change parent to GO:0016668	oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors, NAD(P) as acceptor (EC:1.8.1.-)
- [x] add RHEA:36595 and RHEA:36599 as narrowMatch xref
- [x] update name and def to NAD(P)H to match EC and RHEA


----


id: GO:0052752
name: reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: reduced coenzyme F420 + CoB-S-S-CoM = coenzyme F420 + CoM-SH + CoB-SH." [GOC:mengo_curators, PMID:9914308]
is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors

- [ ] no annotations, no xref. -> suggest obsolete


----


id: GO:0016002
name: sulfite reductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: hydrogen sulfide + acceptor + 3 H2O = sulfite + reduced acceptor." [GOC:curators]
is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors

=> this appears to be an unncessary grouping term for:
GO:0052806 sulfite reductase (coenzyme F420) activity
GO:0004783 sulfite reductase (NADPH) activity

=> there are 8 direct EXP annotations, all to M. tuberculosis 'sir' (P9WJ03)
=> UniProt annotates P9WJ03 to EC:1.8.7.1 = GO:0050311 sulfite reductase (ferredoxin) activity (which ironically, isn't a child of GO:0016002.

- [ ] suggest obsolete GO:0016002, and re-annotate 'sir' (P9WJ03) to GO:0050311


----


id: GO:0070226
name: sulfur:ferric ion oxidoreductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: a perthiol + 4 Fe3+ + 3 H2O = sulfite + a thiol + 4 Fe2+ + 8 H+." [MetaCyc:SULFFEOXIDO-RXN]
xref: MetaCyc:SULFFEOXIDO-RXN
is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors

=> MetaCyc says 'no EC number assigned'

- [x] update def to current MetaCyc reaction "S-sulfanylglutathione + 4 Fe3+ + 3 H2O → sulfite + 4 Fe2+ + glutathione + 6 H+"


----


id: GO:0047950
name: glutathione oxidase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: 2 glutathione + O2 = glutathione disulfide + H2O2." [EC:1.8.3.3, RHEA:24112]
xref: EC:1.8.3.3 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
xref: KEGG_REACTION:R00120
xref: MetaCyc:GLUTATHIONE-OXIDASE-RXN
xref: RHEA:24112 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
is_a: GO:0016670 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors, oxygen as acceptor

- [x] This should be child of (to match RHEA hierarchy):
thiol oxidase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: 2 R'C(R)SH + O2 = R'C(R)S-S(R)CR' + 2 H2O2." [RHEA:17357]
","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mF9GI","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet Can you check to see if you agree with the 2 suggested obsoletions above? thanks.","createdAt":"2026-02-06T12:59:52Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3860320648","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mHDis","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"id: GO:0015035
name: protein-disulfide reductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: a protein with reduced sulfide groups = a protein with oxidized disulfide bonds." [PMID:7559385]
xref: MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN
is_a: GO:0015036 ! disulfide oxidoreductase activity
is_a: GO:0140096 ! catalytic activity, acting on a protein

This term fits into this hierarchy (with xrefs in parentheses):
```
oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors (EC:1.8.-.-)
 |__disulfide oxidoreductase activity (no xrefs)
	|_protein-disulfide reductase activity (MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN => 1.8.4.15)
		|__flavin-dependent sulfhydryl oxidase activity (RHEA:59116)
		|__protein-disulfide reductase (glutathione) activity (EC:1.8.4.2, RHEA:21064)
		|__protein-disulfide reductase [NAD(P)H] activity (EC:1.8.1.8, RHEA:18749, RHEA:18753)
```

Problems here:
- MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN is: [oxidized DsbA protein] + a [protein]-dithiol → [reduced DsbA protein] + a [protein]-disulfide. So is a specific reaction catalysed by bacterial dsbA, which MetaCyc says is a subtype of EC:1.8.4.15 (which has no corresponding GO term). So this isn't an appropriate xref on this GO grouping term, which includes a child term with a EC:1.8.1.8 xref)
- Similarly, the PMID:7559385 xref is for a specific E.coli enzyme, whose activity is like that of DsbA. Title is: "A novel function of Escherichia coli chaperone DnaJ. Protein-disulfide isomerase" and the abstract includes "we show that DnaJ, like thioredoxin, protein-disulfide isomerase, and DsbA, possesses an active dithiol/disulfide group and catalyzes protein disulfide formation". So this isn't a good reference for this GO grouping term either.

- [x] Replace PMID:7559385 with PMID: 18331844 (not a great reference, but better.
- [x] Remove MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN xref

name: sulfite reductase activity

is_a: GO:0016667 ! oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors

=> this appears to be an unncessary grouping term for:

- [ ] suggest obsolete GO:0016002, and re-annotate 'sir' (P9WJ03) to GO:0050311","createdAt":"2026-02-06T17:42:29Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3861777019","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mPtr6","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"GO:0052752 reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity appears to be RHEA:55744 EC 1.8.98.4 coenzyme F420:CoB-CoM heterodisulfide,ferredoxin reductase that has 3 uniprot annotations. Should we still obsolete?","createdAt":"2026-02-06T22:34:21Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3862878970","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mq1vs","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @raymond91125 

I saw that RHEA too, but wasn't sure it was the same reaction.

GO:0052752 is:
name: reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: reduced coenzyme F420 + CoB-S-S-CoM = coenzyme F420 + CoM-SH + CoB-SH." [PMID:9914308], which looked at the archaeon Methanococcus voltae.

RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4 is:
coenzyme B + 2 oxidized coenzyme F420-(gamma-L-Glu)(n) + coenzyme M + 2 reduced [2Fe-2S]-[ferredoxin] + 4 H(+) = coenzyme M-coenzyme B heterodisulfide + 2 reduced coenzyme F420-(gamma-L-Glu)(n) + 2 oxidized [2Fe-2S]-[ferredoxin]
based on PMID: 28174314, which looked at the Archaeon Methanosarcina acetivorans.

That is, the RHEA specifies a Ferredoxin-dependent reaction, seen in a different organism compared to the GO term.

However, given there are no annotations and no xref on the GO:0052752 term, this possible difference in specificity won't matter!
So, yes, I think we could keep GO:0052752 and rename/redefine it based on RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4, also replacing PMID:9914308 with PMID:28174314.


","createdAt":"2026-02-09T08:07:54Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3869989868","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7m8R0C","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@sjm41 You're correct that GO:0052752 is not RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4. To prevent any confusion, I'll go ahead with obsoletion. *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-02-10T00:15:51Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3874590933","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7m8kRa","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Obsoletion of GO:0052752 completed in PR #31538.

**Summary of changes:**
- Term name changed to "obsolete reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity"
- Definition prefixed with "OBSOLETE."
- Synonyms removed (3 total)
- Logical axiom (is_a GO:0016667) removed
- Added `consider: GO:0016667` as a general fallback for users who may encounter this obsolete term
- Comment documenting reason for obsoletion added

The obsoletion follows standard GO practice: since there is no direct EC/RHEA equivalent and no annotations exist, no `replaced_by` is specified. The `consider` tag points to the former parent term as a starting point for finding alternative terms.

Validation passes all 20 SPARQL checks.","createdAt":"2026-02-10T00:29:50Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3874636890","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7m8k1y","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"GO:0052752 "reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity" has been obsoleted as requested.

**Changes made in PR #31538:**
- Prefixed name with "obsolete"
- Prefixed definition with "OBSOLETE."
- Removed 3 synonyms
- Removed is_a relationship to GO:0016667
- Added comment explaining reason: no EC or RHEA xref and no annotations
- Added `consider: GO:0016667` as a general fallback

No `replaced_by` was specified since RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4 was determined to be a different (ferredoxin-dependent) reaction per the discussion above.

All validation checks pass.

","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mF9GI","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet Can you check to see if you agree with the 2 suggested obsoletions above? thanks.","createdAt":"2026-02-06T12:59:52Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3860320648","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mHDis","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"id: GO:0015035
name: protein-disulfide reductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: a protein with reduced sulfide groups = a protein with oxidized disulfide bonds." [PMID:7559385]
xref: MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN
is_a: GO:0015036 ! disulfide oxidoreductase activity
is_a: GO:0140096 ! catalytic activity, acting on a protein

This term fits into this hierarchy (with xrefs in parentheses):
```
oxidoreductase activity, acting on a sulfur group of donors (EC:1.8.-.-)
 |__disulfide oxidoreductase activity (no xrefs)
	|_protein-disulfide reductase activity (MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN => 1.8.4.15)
		|__flavin-dependent sulfhydryl oxidase activity (RHEA:59116)
		|__protein-disulfide reductase (glutathione) activity (EC:1.8.4.2, RHEA:21064)
		|__protein-disulfide reductase [NAD(P)H] activity (EC:1.8.1.8, RHEA:18749, RHEA:18753)
```

Problems here:
- MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN is: [oxidized DsbA protein] + a [protein]-dithiol → [reduced DsbA protein] + a [protein]-disulfide. So is a specific reaction catalysed by bacterial dsbA, which MetaCyc says is a subtype of EC:1.8.4.15 (which has no corresponding GO term). So this isn't an appropriate xref on this GO grouping term, which includes a child term with a EC:1.8.1.8 xref)
- Similarly, the PMID:7559385 xref is for a specific E.coli enzyme, whose activity is like that of DsbA. Title is: "A novel function of Escherichia coli chaperone DnaJ. Protein-disulfide isomerase" and the abstract includes "we show that DnaJ, like thioredoxin, protein-disulfide isomerase, and DsbA, possesses an active dithiol/disulfide group and catalyzes protein disulfide formation". So this isn't a good reference for this GO grouping term either.

- [x] Replace PMID:7559385 with PMID: 18331844 (not a great reference, but better.
- [x] Remove MetaCyc:DISULFOXRED-RXN xref

Should we still obsolete?","createdAt":"2026-02-06T22:34:21Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31518#issuecomment-3862878970","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7mq1vs","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @raymond91125 

I saw that RHEA too, but wasn't sure it was the same reaction.

GO:0052752 is:
name: reduced coenzyme F420:heterodisulfide oxidoreductase activity
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: reduced coenzyme F420 + CoB-S-S-CoM = coenzyme F420 + CoM-SH + CoB-SH." [PMID:9914308], which looked at the archaeon Methanococcus voltae.

RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4 is:
coenzyme B + 2 oxidized coenzyme F420-(gamma-L-Glu)(n) + coenzyme M + 2 reduced [2Fe-2S]-[ferredoxin] + 4 H(+) = coenzyme M-coenzyme B heterodisulfide + 2 reduced coenzyme F420-(gamma-L-Glu)(n) + 2 oxidized [2Fe-2S]-[ferredoxin]
based on PMID: 28174314, which looked at the Archaeon Methanosarcina acetivorans.

That is, the RHEA specifies a Ferredoxin-dependent reaction, seen in a different organism compared to the GO term.

However, given there are no annotations and no xref on the GO:0052752 term, this possible difference in specificity won't matter!
So, yes, I think we could keep GO:0052752 and rename/redefine it based on RHEA:55744/EC 1.8.98.4, also replacing PMID:9914308 with PMID:28174314.
